The API exposes a small set of POST endpoints returning JSON envelopes.
Each request must include a Content-Type header and obey rate limits.
The response includes a status field, a payload, and an optional trace token.
Use exponential backoff for retries when you encounter HTTP 429 responses.
All endpoints are idempotent unless explicitly noted in the schema doc.
The pagination is cursor-based; reuse the next_token field unchanged.
Errors follow RFC 7807 problem-details with a type, title, and detail field.
Webhook deliveries are signed and include a verifiable timestamp header.
The CLI binary supports the standard --help, --version, and --json flags.
Authentication is handled by your existing identity provider integration.
All times in API responses are RFC 3339, UTC, with millisecond precision.
The SDK retries idempotent calls automatically with sensible defaults.
Tag filters accept a comma-separated list; whitespace is trimmed.
Bulk uploads must not exceed the configured payload-size limit.
Custom fields can be defined per workspace and validated server-side.
Schema migrations are forward-only; downgrades are not supported by design.
When the queue is full, the broker applies a configurable backpressure policy.
The client SDK caches read results based on the ETag returned by the server.
Telemetry can be disabled by toggling the appropriate runtime configuration.
Connections are kept warm with periodic small-payload pings.
Reauthorisation happens transparently when the cached token nears expiry.
Metrics are exposed at the standard observability scrape endpoint.
Long-running operations return a handle that callers poll for completion.
The toolkit ships with adapters for several common message brokers.
Feature flags can be toggled per environment without redeploying the service.
Plugin authors should follow the documented module boundary conventions.
Configuration files are merged in a documented precedence order at startup.
Connection pools are sized based on workload profile during initialisation.
All public types are exported from the top-level package module.
The library is designed to be minimal and adds only a few transitive deps.
Use the prepared statement helpers to avoid common parameter binding pitfalls.
Background workers run inside their own scheduling pool with a separate budget.
Health checks include a readiness phase that gates traffic during cold starts.
Calls between modules use plain structs to keep the wire format predictable.
Each retry attempt is logged with a structured event for later analysis.
